goal: After the first permit-gated sandbox write has been proven, close the remaining legacy write bypasses without removing Maya's future autonomy. Retire POST /probe/sandbox-real-write permanently with HTTP 410, regardless of its old env flag, and put POST /probe/sandbox-write-check behind a new explicit diagnostic flag BLUEPILOT_SANDBOX_WRITE_CHECK_ENABLED. Real content writes should continue only through the permit/policy path, where future Maya policy can issue one-shot permits directly inside an approved scope.
scope_out: Do not delete the legacy implementation wholesale; make the retired path unreachable with a small, clear 410 response.
Do not change /probe/sandbox-permit-write or the permit-gated write path.
Do not change maya-core, permit registers, corridor policy, GitHub write adapters, target profiles, provider code, or package dependencies.
Do not remove future Maya autonomy. Future direct writes by Maya must happen through policy-issued one-shot permits, not through legacy bypass endpoints.
Do not set or change Render environment values in code.
claims: POST /probe/sandbox-real-write returns HTTP 410 with a retired/replacement message even if BLUEPILOT_SANDBOX_REAL_WRITE_ENABLED is true, and it never calls orchestrateTask.
POST /probe/sandbox-write-check is closed by default with HTTP 403 unless BLUEPILOT_SANDBOX_WRITE_CHECK_ENABLED is exactly true.
When BLUEPILOT_SANDBOX_WRITE_CHECK_ENABLED is true, sandbox-write-check keeps the existing confirmation phrase, fixed sandbox target, token secrecy, create-and-delete behavior, and status payload.
No automated local caller for /probe/sandbox-write-check exists in the current tree; contracts, docs, and tests are the only references.
The permit-gated path from BP-146 remains the only real content-write probe path and is not modified by this task.
assumptions: bluepilot/main is at a35df3c after BP-147.
The first permit-gated write has been proven and the bluepilot permit write window is closed.
Future Maya self-directed writes will be implemented by policy/permit issuance, so this task should remove bypasses without weakening that intended direction.
dod: builder/src/sandboxRealWrite.ts returns 410 for /probe/sandbox-real-write before reading env, body, profile, or orchestrator.
builder/src/sandboxWriteProbe.ts checks BLUEPILOT_SANDBOX_WRITE_CHECK_ENABLED before reading body or touching GitHub.
Tests prove real-write stays retired with the old flag on and off, and write-check does not call GitHub while its new flag is off.
Tests prove write-check still works with the new flag on and the confirmation phrase present.
STATE.md and docs/SESSION-LOG.md record BP-148 and preserve the future policy/permit path for Maya autonomy.
review-packets/BP-148.md records scope, safety invariants, no automated write-check caller, and verification.
npm test and npm run typecheck in builder/ pass.
stop_conditions: Any change to permit-gated write behavior becomes necessary.
Any package or dependency change becomes necessary.
Any automated caller for sandbox-write-check is found and would need coordinated runtime changes.
prior_task_findings: BP-147 documented the first real permit-gated sandbox write and the closed state afterward. BP-148 follows by defusing legacy write bypasses while keeping the intended future path: Maya may later write directly within policy by issuing one-shot permits, not by using legacy probe endpoints.
